company.segment_en: Wafer-scale AI compute — the pure-play bet on the training→inference shift, newly public (Nasdaq, May 2026), and TSMC's most wafer-intensive customer per chip
company.segment_zh: 晶圓級 AI 算力 — 押注「訓練→推論」轉移的純標的，2026/5 剛於 Nasdaq 上市，也是台積電「每顆晶片最吃晶圓」的客戶
company.blurb_en: Four pillars on the wafer-scale inference challenger: how Cerebras raises realized compute price (locked-in backlog · latency-as-product · wafer-scale moat) and where the token price-war and margin compression push back · what to watch it with · how much it makes from AI (≈ all of its ~$0.9B revenue, atop a $24.6B backlog) · and how ~100% of it is a single TSMC N5 wafer — uniquely CoWoS/HBM-light.
company.blurb_zh: 對「晶圓級推論挑戰者」的四支柱：Cerebras 怎麼提高已實現算力售價（鎖定的在手訂單 · 速度即產品 · 晶圓級護城河），以及代幣價格戰與毛利壓縮如何反推 · 用什麼資料觀察 · 從 AI 賺多少（≈ 其全部約 $0.9B 營收，背後是 $24.6B 在手訂單）· 以及這一切如何幾乎 100% 是一整片台積電 N5 晶圓 — 且罕見地「不吃 CoWoS／HBM」。

thesis_en: Cerebras is the purest public bet on the training→inference shift. Its Wafer-Scale Engine — a single TSMC 5nm wafer, ~57x the die of an H100 with 44GB of on-chip SRAM — turns LATENCY into the product, posting inference speeds (Llama 8B ~1,800 tok/s) GPUs struggle to match. The bull case is demand, not unit price: a $24.6B RPO backlog (~48x 2025 revenue) plus a >$20B / 750MW OpenAI commitment and DOE/national-lab anchors. The honest tension is the other side of the ledger — realized economics are UNDER PRESSURE (gross margin guided down from 47% to 36-38%), the token price-war is brutal, and 86% of revenue comes from two related UAE entities (MBZUAI + G42). For TSMC, Cerebras is a different shape of customer: it consumes raw advanced-node WAFER AREA at scale yet largely bypasses the CoWoS + HBM bottleneck — so its binding constraint is data-center POWER and its own deployment capex, not packaging.
thesis_zh: Cerebras 是公開市場上對「訓練→推論」轉移最純粹的押注。它的晶圓級引擎（Wafer-Scale Engine）— 一整片台積電 5nm 晶圓、晶粒約是 H100 的 57 倍、片上 44GB SRAM — 把「延遲」變成產品，推出 GPU 難以匹敵的推論速度（Llama 8B 約 1,800 tok/s）。多頭論點在需求、不在單位價格：$24.6B 在手訂單（RPO，約 2025 營收的 48 倍）、加上 OpenAI >$20B／750MW 承諾與 DOE／國家實驗室錨定客戶。誠實的張力在帳本另一邊 — 已實現經濟正受壓（毛利指引從 47% 砍到 36-38%）、代幣價格戰殘酷，且 86% 營收來自兩家阿聯酋關係企業（MBZUAI＋G42）。對台積電而言，Cerebras 是另一種形狀的客戶：它大量消耗先進製程的「晶圓面積」，卻幾乎繞過 CoWoS＋HBM 瓶頸 — 因此它的綁定約束是資料中心電力與自身部署資本支出，而非封裝。

headline.verdict_zh: HOLDING — 已實現價格大致持平

pricing.verdict_zh: HOLDING — 已實現價格大致持平

levers.name_en: Locked-in backlog & multi-year commitments (RPO / OpenAI / G42)
levers.name_zh: 鎖定的在手訂單與多年期承諾（RPO／OpenAI／G42）
levers.mechanism_en: Cerebras carries a $24.6B remaining-performance-obligation backlog (~48x 2025 revenue), a >$20B OpenAI master agreement (750MW, options to 2GW) and G42 take-or-pay commitments (~$1.43B, $640M prepaid). These lock forward volume and pricing across multiple years, giving Cerebras pricing visibility and reference points few startups have — the single strongest pillar under its realized price.
levers.mechanism_zh: Cerebras 握有 $24.6B 剩餘履約義務（RPO，約 2025 營收的 48 倍）、OpenAI >$20B 主協議（750MW、選擇權至 2GW）與 G42 take-or-pay 承諾（約 $1.43B、已預付 $640M）。這些跨多年鎖定前瞻量與價格，給 Cerebras 少數新創才有的定價能見度與參考點 — 是其已實現價格底下最強的一根支柱。
levers.observability_en: Cerebras 10-Q RPO/backlog line, OpenAI/G42/AWS contract disclosures, deferred-revenue & prepayment balances.
levers.observability_zh: Cerebras 10-Q 的 RPO／在手訂單項、OpenAI／G42／AWS 合約揭露、遞延收入與預付款餘額。
levers.source_en: Cerebras S-1 / 10-Q; OpenAI & G42 deal announcements
levers.source_zh: Cerebras S-1／10-Q；OpenAI 與 G42 合約公告

levers.name_zh: 「速度即產品」溢價（延遲就是規格）
levers.mechanism_en: Wafer-scale on-chip memory bandwidth (~21 PB/s) lets Cerebras post inference speeds GPUs can't — ~1,800 tok/s on Llama 8B, ~450 tok/s on 70B, ~969 tok/s on 405B. For real-time and agentic workloads where tokens/sec IS the product (Mistral Le Chat, Perplexity, AlphaSense), that latency commands a premium and lets Cerebras price above raw cost-per-token. The edge is strongest on small/mid models and contested on the largest.
levers.mechanism_zh: 晶圓級片上記憶體頻寬（約 21 PB/s）讓 Cerebras 跑出 GPU 做不到的推論速度 — Llama 8B 約 1,800 tok/s、70B 約 450 tok/s、405B 約 969 tok/s。對「每秒代幣數即產品」的即時與代理（agentic）工作負載（Mistral Le Chat、Perplexity、AlphaSense），這份延遲帶來溢價，讓 Cerebras 能在純成本之上定價。優勢在中小模型最強，在最大模型則受競爭。
levers.observability_en: Artificial Analysis & Cerebras benchmark tokens/sec, published $/M-token pricing, named latency-sensitive customer wins.
levers.observability_zh: Artificial Analysis 與 Cerebras 的 tok/s 基準、公布的每百萬代幣價格、延遲敏感客戶的具名勝出。

levers.name_zh: 晶圓級架構護城河（無直接替代品）
levers.mechanism_en: A 4-trillion-transistor, 900,000-core single wafer with 44GB on-chip SRAM is an architecture no merchant GPU replicates — the whole model fits on-chip, eliminating chip-to-chip overhead. This uniqueness insulates pricing on workloads that need it: there is no second-source to bid Cerebras down, unlike a fungible GPU rack.
levers.mechanism_zh: 4 兆電晶體、90 萬核心、片上 44GB SRAM 的單一晶圓，是商用 GPU 無法複製的架構 — 整個模型放進片上，消除晶片間通訊負擔。這份獨特性在需要它的工作負載上保護定價：不像可替換的 GPU 機櫃，沒有第二供應商能把 Cerebras 殺價。
levers.observability_en: WSE-3 spec disclosures, win/loss vs GPU clusters on latency-bound RFPs, competitive teardown commentary.
levers.observability_zh: WSE-3 規格揭露、延遲綁定標案中對 GPU 叢集的勝負、競品拆解評論。

levers.name_zh: 錨定合約的可信度（OpenAI／DOE／國家實驗室）
levers.mechanism_en: Marquee anchors — the OpenAI master agreement, a DOE 'Genesis Mission' MOU, and deployed CS-3 systems at Sandia / Lawrence Livermore / Los Alamos — act as pricing references and credibility that pull in commercial logos at firmer terms. Validation by the most demanding buyers supports realized price across the rest of the book.
levers.mechanism_zh: 招牌錨定客戶 — OpenAI 主協議、DOE「Genesis Mission」MOU、以及部署於 Sandia／Lawrence Livermore／Los Alamos 的 CS-3 系統 — 作為定價參考與可信度，以更穩的條件帶進商業客戶。被最挑剔買家驗證，支撐其餘訂單的已實現價格。
levers.observability_en: New anchor announcements, government/national-lab deployment count, commercial customer logo additions.
levers.observability_zh: 新錨定客戶公告、政府／國家實驗室部署數、新增商業客戶。
levers.source_en: Cerebras press releases; DOE / national-lab announcements
levers.source_zh: Cerebras 新聞稿；DOE／國家實驗室公告

levers.name_zh: 代幣價格戰（已實現價格拖累）
levers.mechanism_en: Inference is commoditizing fast: per-token prices have collapsed (~$20 → ~$0.07 per million tokens since 2022) and Gartner projects another ~90% decline by 2030. Cerebras must price its inference cloud against hyperscaler and Groq/SambaNova rates, so even with a speed premium its realized price-per-token is dragged down — a structural headwind on the up-stack levers above.
levers.mechanism_zh: 推論正快速商品化：每代幣價格崩跌（2022 至今每百萬代幣約 $20 → $0.07），Gartner 預估到 2030 再降約 90%。Cerebras 的推論雲必須對齊雲端巨頭與 Groq／SambaNova 的價格，因此即便有速度溢價，其每代幣已實現價格仍被往下拖 — 是上述上移槓桿之上的結構性逆風。
levers.observability_en: Published $/M-token across providers, Cerebras Inference price changes, Stanford AI Index cost-decline series.
levers.observability_zh: 各供應商公布的每百萬代幣價格、Cerebras Inference 價格變動、史丹佛 AI Index 成本下降序列。
levers.source_en: Provider pricing pages; Stanford AI Index 2025
levers.source_zh: 各供應商定價頁；史丹佛 AI Index 2025

levers.name_zh: 晶圓級單位經濟壓縮（已實現利潤拖累）
levers.mechanism_en: The Q1 2026 print guided gross margin DOWN from 46.5% to 36-38% (full-year 38-41%, operating margin (28-32%)). Wafer-scale uses enormous silicon area per model (a 70B model reportedly spans multiple wafers), and the backlog ramp is gated by data-center power and Cerebras's own deployment capex — so as volume scales, realized economics per unit of compute are under pressure, not expanding.
levers.mechanism_zh: 2026 Q1 財報把毛利指引從 46.5% 砍到 36-38%（全年 38-41%、營益率 (28-32%)）。晶圓級每個模型吃下巨量矽面積（一個 70B 模型據報需跨多片晶圓），且在手訂單放量受資料中心電力與 Cerebras 自身部署資本支出制約 — 因此隨量擴張，每單位算力的已實現經濟是受壓、而非擴張。
levers.observability_en: Quarterly gross-margin actuals vs guide, deployment capex & power commentary, utilization of installed CS-3 fleet.
levers.observability_zh: 每季毛利實績 vs 指引、部署資本支出與電力評論、已裝機 CS-3 機隊的利用率。
levers.source_en: Cerebras Q1 2026 results & guidance; earnings call
levers.source_zh: Cerebras 2026 Q1 財報與指引；法說會

pricing.engine_note_en: the pricing power is locked-in backlog and the latency-as-product premium — not unit-price expansion, where the token price-war and wafer-scale economics are compressing realized margin
pricing.engine_note_zh: 定價權來自鎖定的在手訂單與「速度即產品」溢價 — 而非單位價格擴張；在單位價格端，代幣價格戰與晶圓級經濟正壓縮已實現利潤

items.what_en: Revenue, gross margin, RPO/backlog, customer concentration (MBZUAI/G42), deferred revenue — the hard floor under every pillar. Newly public, so history is short.
items.what_zh: 營收、毛利、RPO／在手訂單、客戶集中度（MBZUAI／G42）、遞延收入 — 各支柱的硬底。剛上市，歷史尚短。

items.name_zh: Cerebras 法說會與投資人關係
items.what_en: Guided core revenue & gross-margin trajectory, OpenAI / AWS / DOE deal commentary, deployment & power constraints — the swing factors on backlog conversion.
items.what_zh: 指引的核心營收與毛利軌跡、OpenAI／AWS／DOE 合約評論、部署與電力約束 — 在手訂單轉換的擺盪因子。

items.name_zh: CBRS 股價（Nasdaq）＋分析師目標價
items.what_en: Live price, market cap, P/S vs NVDA/AVGO, consensus target (~$299) and lockup calendar — the market's read on backlog-conversion odds. Sentiment context, kept OUT of the pricing-power score.
items.what_zh: 即時價格、市值、P/S vs NVDA／AVGO、共識目標價（約 $299）與解禁時程 — 市場對在手訂單轉換機率的判讀。屬情緒情境，刻意排除在定價權分數外。

items.name_zh: 推論基準（Artificial Analysis、Cerebras 部落格）
items.what_en: Independent tokens/sec and $/M-token across models — the cross-check on the latency-as-product lever vs Groq / SambaNova / NVIDIA.
items.what_zh: 跨模型的獨立 tok/s 與每百萬代幣價格 — 對「速度即產品」槓桿、相對 Groq／SambaNova／NVIDIA 的交叉驗證。

items.name_zh: 台積電營收與先進製程揭露
items.what_en: N5/N3 wafer mix and capacity — the raw wafer supply behind every WSE; note Cerebras is wafer-heavy but CoWoS-light, unlike NVIDIA/Broadcom.
items.what_zh: N5／N3 晶圓結構與產能 — 每片 WSE 背後的原始晶圓供給；注意 Cerebras 吃晶圓但少用 CoWoS，與 NVIDIA／Broadcom 不同。

items.name_en: Customer-concentration & anchor disclosures (G42 / MBZUAI / OpenAI / DOE)
items.name_zh: 客戶集中度與錨定揭露（G42／MBZUAI／OpenAI／DOE）
items.what_en: Related-party revenue share (~86% UAE), OpenAI MRA & warrant terms, DOE/national-lab deployments — the demand anchors AND the concentration risk.
items.what_zh: 關係人營收占比（約 86% 阿聯酋）、OpenAI 主協議與認股權條件、DOE／國家實驗室部署 — 既是需求錨定，也是集中度風險。

items.what_en: Independent estimates of wafer-scale cost-per-token and silicon-area-per-model — the cross-check on the margin-compression lever.
items.what_zh: 晶圓級每代幣成本與每模型矽面積的獨立估計 — 對毛利壓縮槓桿的交叉驗證。

items.name_zh: AI 推論市場與代幣價格追蹤
items.what_en: Inference market size (~$106B 2025 → ~$255B 2030 EST), inference share of AI compute (~2/3 by 2026), and token-price indices — the demand backdrop and the price-war.
items.what_zh: 推論市場規模（約 $106B 2025 → 約 $255B 2030 EST）、推論占 AI 算力比重（2026 約 2/3）、代幣價格指數 — 需求背景與價格戰。

estimates.method_en: Total revenue run-rate (guided FY2026 core revenue)
estimates.method_zh: 總營收年化（指引 FY2026 核心營收）
estimates.basis_en: Cerebras is a pure-play AI compute company, so total revenue ≈ AI benefit. 2025 revenue was $510M (+76%); Q1 2026 was $193.4M (+92%); management guides FY2026 core revenue to ~$855-865M. This is a disclosed/guided figure — only the annualization is a projection. For the demand-side coverage view see /payback; for the inference-share backdrop see /compute.
estimates.basis_zh: Cerebras 是純 AI 算力公司，故總營收 ≈ AI 利益。2025 營收 $510M（+76%）；2026 Q1 為 $193.4M（+92%）；管理層指引 FY2026 核心營收約 $855-865M。此為揭露／指引值 — 唯一推估是年化。需求端覆蓋率視角見 /payback；推論占比背景見 /compute。

estimates.method_zh: 在手訂單（剩餘履約義務 RPO）
estimates.basis_en: S-1 discloses $24.6B of RPO — ~48x 2025 revenue. Recognition is back-loaded: ~15% (~$3.7B) through 2027, ~43% (~$10.6B) in 2028-2029, ~42% after. This is contracted future revenue, NOT an annual figure — backlog conversion timing (gated by power & deployment) is the central swing factor. Cancellability and related-party terms are the caveats.
estimates.basis_zh: S-1 揭露 $24.6B 的 RPO — 約 2025 營收的 48 倍。認列後段集中：到 2027 約 15%（約 $3.7B）、2028-2029 約 43%（約 $10.6B）、其後約 42%。這是已簽約的未來營收、非年度數字 — 在手訂單的轉換時點（受電力與部署制約）是核心擺盪因子。可取消性與關係人條件是但書。

estimates.basis_en: The Dec 2025 OpenAI master agreement is reported at >$20B for 750MW of capacity (options to 2GW), bundled with a warrant to OpenAI (~33.4M shares) and a $1B working-capital loan to Cerebras. The headline TCV is disclosed-ish but figure precision and vesting milestones (incl. a $40B market-cap trigger) make the realized value an estimate.
estimates.basis_zh: 2025/12 的 OpenAI 主協議報導 >$20B、對應 750MW 產能（選擇權至 2GW），並綁定給 OpenAI 的認股權（約 33.4M 股）與給 Cerebras 的 $1B 營運資金貸款。頭條 TCV 大致揭露，但金額精度與行權里程碑（含 $40B 市值門檻）使已實現價值仍屬估計。

benefit.disclosure_note_en: Cerebras reports total revenue directly and it ≈ AI benefit (a pure-play). The headline is the guided ~$0.86B FY2026 core run-rate; the $24.6B RPO backlog and >$20B OpenAI commitment are disclosed CONTRACTED figures, not annual revenue — backlog-conversion timing is the swing factor, and 86% of revenue is two related UAE entities.
benefit.disclosure_note_zh: Cerebras 直接揭露總營收、且 ≈ AI 利益（純標的）。頭條為指引的約 $0.86B FY2026 核心年化；$24.6B RPO 在手訂單與 >$20B OpenAI 承諾是已揭露的「已簽約」數字、非年度營收 — 在手訂單轉換時點是擺盪因子，且 86% 營收來自兩家阿聯酋關係企業。

chain.role_en: The product = one full TSMC wafer (training + inference)
chain.role_zh: 產品本身＝一整片台積電晶圓（訓練＋推論）
chain.note_en: 4T transistors, 900K cores, 44GB on-chip SRAM on a 46,225mm² die — ~57x an H100. The entire chip is a single TSMC N5 wafer: the most wafer-intensive product TSMC builds per unit.
chain.note_zh: 4 兆電晶體、90 萬核心、46,225mm² 晶粒上 44GB 片上 SRAM — 約 H100 的 57 倍。整顆晶片就是一整片台積電 N5 晶圓：台積電「每顆最吃晶圓」的產品。

chain.role_en: Deployed appliance — power, cooling, I/O around the wafer
chain.role_zh: 部署整機 — 晶圓外的電力、散熱、I/O
chain.note_en: The shippable unit. Its constraint is data-center POWER and cooling, not packaging — the very thing gating backlog conversion and compressing margins.
chain.note_zh: 可出貨單位。其約束是資料中心電力與散熱、而非封裝 — 正是卡住在手訂單轉換、壓縮毛利的那個變數。

chain.note_en: The roadmap pushes Cerebras onto TSMC's most advanced node — deepening leading-edge wafer dependence as it scales. Node/timing is industry-attributed.
chain.note_zh: 路線圖把 Cerebras 推上台積電最先進節點 — 隨擴張加深對先進製程晶圓的依賴。節點／時程為產業歸因。

chain.role_en: Streams model weights to the wafer (disaggregated memory)
chain.role_zh: 把模型權重串流到晶圓（解耦記憶體）
chain.note_en: Disaggregated DRAM appliance that feeds weights — this is how Cerebras sidesteps HBM: commodity DRAM + streaming instead of HBM stacks + CoWoS.
chain.note_zh: 餵入權重的解耦 DRAM 整機 — 這正是 Cerebras 繞過 HBM 的方式：商品 DRAM＋串流，取代 HBM 堆疊＋CoWoS。

chain.role_en: Links multiple CS-3 into a cluster
chain.role_zh: 把多台 CS-3 連成叢集
chain.note_en: Broadcast/reduce fabric for weight streaming across many wafers — Cerebras's answer to NVLink/InfiniBand scale-out, also on TSMC silicon.
chain.note_zh: 跨多片晶圓做權重串流的 broadcast/reduce fabric — Cerebras 對 NVLink／InfiniBand scale-out 的解法，同樣跑在台積電矽上。

chain.accelerator_zh: 晶圓級整合與冗餘核心良率架構
chain.role_en: What makes a whole-wafer chip yield-able & manufacturable
chain.role_zh: 讓整片晶圓晶片能量產、可良率化
chain.note_en: Co-engineered redundancy, power delivery and the wafer-scale process flow with TSMC — the manufacturing moat that makes a single-wafer chip possible at all.
chain.note_zh: 與台積電共同工程的冗餘、供電與晶圓級製程流程 — 讓「單一晶圓晶片」得以成立的製造護城河。

silicon.tsmc_read_en: Essentially 100% of Cerebras's silicon is fabbed by TSMC — WSE-3 on N5, the WSE-4 roadmap on N3, MemoryX/SwarmX on N7. But Cerebras is a DIFFERENT shape of customer from NVIDIA or Broadcom: its entire product is a single ~46,225mm² wafer (~57x an H100 die), so it consumes raw advanced-node WAFER AREA at extraordinary intensity per unit — yet it largely BYPASSES the CoWoS + HBM bottleneck (on-wafer SRAM + streamed commodity DRAM instead of HBM stacks and chip-to-chip CoWoS). For TSMC that means pure wafer-start demand decoupled from the packaging constraint that gates NVIDIA/Broadcom. As Cerebras converts its $24.6B backlog and the OpenAI 750MW→2GW ramp, that is incremental N5/N3 wafer demand — but its binding constraint is data-center POWER and its own deployment capex, the very thing that just compressed its margins, not CoWoS.
silicon.tsmc_read_zh: Cerebras 的矽幾乎 100% 由台積電製造 — WSE-3 用 N5、WSE-4 路線圖用 N3、MemoryX／SwarmX 用 N7。但 Cerebras 是與 NVIDIA 或 Broadcom 不同形狀的客戶：它的整個產品就是一整片約 46,225mm² 的晶圓（約 H100 晶粒的 57 倍），因此每單位以驚人強度消耗先進製程「晶圓面積」 — 卻幾乎繞過 CoWoS＋HBM 瓶頸（片上 SRAM＋串流商品 DRAM，取代 HBM 堆疊與晶片間 CoWoS）。對台積電而言，這是與「卡住 NVIDIA／Broadcom 的封裝約束」脫鉤的純晶圓投片需求。當 Cerebras 轉換 $24.6B 在手訂單與 OpenAI 750MW→2GW 放量，那是增量的 N5／N3 晶圓需求 — 但它的綁定約束是資料中心電力與自身部署資本支出（正是剛壓縮其毛利的那個變數），而非 CoWoS。

proxies.name_zh: 台積電（TSM）— 晶圓供給 proxy

alerts.en: GPU-scarcity proxy cooling: NVDA -11.83%/m — watch the accelerator-scarcity premium (capacity-block pricing) for follow-through.
alerts.zh: GPU 稀缺 proxy 降溫：NVDA -11.83%/月 — 留意加速器稀缺溢價（capacity-block 定價）是否跟跌。

alerts.en: Concentration: ~100% of the AI compute behind this benefit is TSMC-fabbed, 3 link(s) critical — the CoWoS bottleneck TSMC controls is the binding constraint on growth.
alerts.zh: 集中度：撐起此利益的 AI 算力約 100% 由台積電製造，3 條鏈關鍵 — 台積電掌握的 CoWoS 瓶頸即成長綁定約束。

alerts.en: AI benefit (headline) ≈ $0.86B revenue_runrate — Cerebras reports total revenue directly and it ≈ AI benefit (a pure-play). The headline is the guided ~$0.86B FY2026 core run-rate; the $24.6B RPO backlog and >$20B OpenAI commitment are disclosed CONTRACTED figures, not annual revenue — backlog-conversion timing is the swing factor, and 86% of revenue is two related UAE entities.
alerts.zh: AI 利益（頭條）≈ $0.86B revenue_runrate — Cerebras 直接揭露總營收、且 ≈ AI 利益（純標的）。頭條為指引的約 $0.86B FY2026 核心年化；$24.6B RPO 在手訂單與 >$20B OpenAI 承諾是已揭露的「已簽約」數字、非年度營收 — 在手訂單轉換時點是擺盪因子，且 86% 營收來自兩家阿聯酋關係企業。

integrated_thesis.en: Cerebras is a wafer-scale inference pure-play whose realized-price engine is not list price but locked-in backlog: $24.6B RPO and a ~$20B OpenAI TCV underwrite a guided ~$0.86B FY2026 run-rate. Speed-as-SKU (latency premium) and a no-substitute wafer-scale moat hold pricing power at 57.7/100, but a token price-war and ramp-cost margin compression drag it. AI benefit ≈ total revenue — it's a pure-play — but 86% flows from two UAE-related entities. Every accelerator (WSE-3/CS-3 on N5, WSE-4 on N3) is ~100% TSMC-fabbed. For TSMC, Cerebras is a direct, wafer-scale, dice-nothing advanced-node customer that bypasses CoWoS entirely — pure wafer volume, not packaging.
integrated_thesis.zh: Cerebras是晶圓級推論純玩家，其實現價格引擎不在標價而在鎖定的積壓訂單：246億美元RPO與約200億美元OpenAI合約價撐起FY2026約8.6億美元的營收流速。以速度為SKU的延遲溢價與無替代品的晶圓級護城河支撐定價力至57.7/100，但代幣價格戰與爬坡成本壓縮拖累之。AI效益約等於總營收——為純玩家——但86%來自兩家UAE關聯實體。每顆加速器（WSE-3/CS-3用N5、WSE-4用N3）近100%由台積電代工。對台積電而言，Cerebras是直接的晶圓級先進製程客戶，且不切割、完全繞過CoWoS——純晶圓量而非封裝。

pricing.en: Score 57.7 HOLDING. The engine is backlog_commitment_lockin (strength 80, T1): multi-year RPO/OpenAI/G42 commitments lock realized price ahead of the token price-war. Latency-as-product (72) lets speed be the SKU at premium. Both T1 drags — token price-war (34) and unit-economics compression (30) — pull margin toward 36-38%. Net: pricing power holds via contracts, not spot.
pricing.zh: 評分57.7，持穩。引擎為積壓承諾鎖定（強度80，T1）：多年期RPO/OpenAI/G42承諾在代幣價格戰前鎖定實現價格。延遲即產品（72）讓速度成為溢價SKU。兩項T1拖累——代幣價格戰（34）與單位經濟壓縮（30）——將毛利拉向36-38%。淨結果：定價力靠合約而非現貨維持。

sources.en: Highest-signal: Cerebras S-1/10-Q/10-K on EDGAR for RPO and customer-concentration disclosure; earnings calls for backlog-conversion cadence; Artificial Analysis for latency-SKU verification; SemiAnalysis inference-cost models to test margin claims; TSMC advanced-node disclosures for N5/N3 wafer allocation. Track anchor filings (G42/MBZUAI/OpenAI/DOE) for concentration.
sources.zh: 最高訊號：EDGAR上的S-1/10-Q/10-K查RPO與客戶集中揭露；財報電話會議查積壓轉換節奏；Artificial Analysis驗證延遲SKU；SemiAnalysis推論成本模型檢驗毛利說法；台積電先進製程揭露查N5/N3晶圓配置。追蹤錨定客戶申報（G42/MBZUAI/OpenAI/DOE）看集中度。

benefit.en: Headline ≈ $0.86B revenue_runrate (guided FY2026 core, high confidence, T1); consensus $0.9B. As a pure-play, AI benefit ≈ total revenue. The $24.6B RPO and >$20B OpenAI TCV are CONTRACTED figures, not annual revenue — backlog-conversion timing is the swing factor. Risk: 86% concentrated in two UAE-related entities; a single anchor slip resets the trajectory.
benefit.zh: 頭條約8.6億美元營收流速（FY2026核心指引，高信心，T1）；共識9億。作為純玩家，AI效益約等於總營收。246億RPO與逾200億OpenAI合約價為已簽約數字，非年度營收——積壓轉換時點為擺盪因素。風險：86%集中於兩家UAE關聯實體；單一錨定客戶失落即重設軌跡。

silicon.en: ~100% TSMC-fabbed, 3 critical links. WSE-3/CS-3 on N5, WSE-4 roadmap on N3 — all wafer-scale, meaning no dicing and no CoWoS. This is the key nuance: Cerebras consumes full wafers as monolithic engines, not chiplets, so it is a pure advanced-node volume play with zero CoWoS demand. MemoryX/SwarmX add N7/N5 support silicon.
silicon.zh: 近100%台積電代工，3條關鍵鏈。WSE-3/CS-3用N5，WSE-4路線圖用N3——皆為晶圓級，即不切割、不需CoWoS。此為關鍵差異：Cerebras以整片晶圓做單片引擎而非小晶片，故為純先進製程晶圓量客戶，CoWoS需求為零。MemoryX/SwarmX增加N7/N5輔助矽。

tsmc_implication.en: Cerebras is a DIRECT advanced-node customer, not indirect-via-NVIDIA. Uniquely, it consumes full N5/N3 wafers as monolithic wafer-scale engines — no dicing, no CoWoS. So it adds leading-edge wafer volume but does NOT compete for TSMC's CoWoS bottleneck; if anything it's the counter-case to the CoWoS constraint. Binding constraint for Cerebras is its own deployment capex and data-center power, plus UAE concentration — not TSMC capacity. Upside for TSMC is modest N3 wafer volume if WSE-4 and the OpenAI ramp convert; downside is a niche customer whose backlog may slip to 2027+.
tsmc_implication.zh: Cerebras是直接先進製程客戶，非經NVIDIA間接。獨特之處在於它以整片N5/N3晶圓做單片晶圓級引擎——不切割、不需CoWoS。故它增加先進製程晶圓量，但不爭奪台積電CoWoS瓶頸；反而是CoWoS限制的反例。Cerebras的約束在於自身部署資本支出、資料中心電力及UAE集中度，而非台積電產能。台積電的上行為WSE-4與OpenAI爬坡轉換帶來的適度N3晶圓量；下行為積壓可能滑至2027+的利基客戶。

scenarios.trigger_en: RPO converts on schedule, OpenAI 750MW + AWS deploy, run-rate clears $2-3B and non-UAE logos cut MBZUAI+G42 concentration below 60%.
scenarios.trigger_zh: RPO按期轉換，OpenAI 750MW+AWS部署，營收流速突破20-30億美元，非UAE客戶將MBZUAI+G42集中度降至60%以下。

scenarios.trigger_en: Gross margin recovers from 36-38% back toward 45%+ as utilization rises, proving Q1 compression was transitory ramp cost.
scenarios.trigger_zh: 隨利用率上升毛利從36-38%回升至45%+，證明Q1壓縮為暫時性爬坡成本。

scenarios.name_zh: 電力與部署瓶頸封頂積壓
scenarios.trigger_en: Data-center power and own deploy capex gate conversion; revenue slips to 2027+, margins stay pinned by ramp cost.
scenarios.trigger_zh: 資料中心電力與自身部署資本支出限制轉換；營收滑至2027+，毛利被爬坡成本壓住。

scenarios.trigger_en: Token price-war + NVIDIA(Groq)/Blackwell/custom ASICs match latency at lower cost; moat narrows, margins stay compressed below 38%.
scenarios.trigger_zh: 代幣價格戰+NVIDIA(Groq)/Blackwell/客製ASIC以更低成本匹配延遲；護城河收窄，毛利壓在38%以下。

falsification.en: A UAE anchor (G42/MBZUAI) cancels or defers, cutting RPO materially and exposing the 86% concentration as the true revenue base — backlog thesis breaks.
falsification.zh: UAE錨定客戶（G42/MBZUAI）取消或延遲，大幅削減RPO並暴露86%集中度為真實營收基礎——積壓論述破裂。

falsification.en: Gross margin stays pinned below 38% through FY2026 despite scaling, proving compression is structural price erosion, not transitory ramp cost.
falsification.zh: 儘管規模擴大，毛利在FY2026仍壓在38%以下，證明壓縮為結構性價格侵蝕而非暫時性爬坡成本。

falsification.en: Independent benchmarks show NVIDIA/Groq/custom ASICs matching Cerebras latency at lower $/token — the latency-SKU premium collapses.
falsification.zh: 獨立基準顯示NVIDIA/Groq/客製ASIC以更低美元/代幣匹配Cerebras延遲——延遲SKU溢價崩解。

watch.en: NVDA inference-competition proxy (-11.83%/m) — scarcity-premium follow-through signals whether capacity-block pricing holds.
watch.zh: NVDA推論競爭代理（-11.83%/月）——稀缺溢價後續動向顯示產能塊定價是否維持。

watch.en: Artificial Analysis inference latency/$-per-token benchmarks vs NVIDIA/Groq — earliest signal of moat erosion.
watch.zh: Artificial Analysis推論延遲與美元/代幣基準對比NVIDIA/Groq——護城河侵蝕最早訊號。

watch.en: RPO backlog-conversion cadence and revenue run-rate vs $0.86B guide in 10-Q filings.
watch.zh: 10-Q中RPO積壓轉換節奏與營收流速對比8.6億指引。

watch.en: Customer-concentration disclosure — non-UAE logo additions cutting MBZUAI+G42 below 60%.
watch.zh: 客戶集中度揭露——新增非UAE客戶將MBZUAI+G42降至60%以下。

watch.en: Gross margin trajectory (36-38% → 45%+) as deployments scale.
watch.zh: 隨部署規模擴大毛利軌跡（36-38%→45%+）。

watch.en: WSE-4 N3 tape-out and TSMC advanced-node wafer allocation — direct TSMC volume signal.
watch.zh: WSE-4 N3流片與台積電先進製程晶圓配置——直接台積電量能訊號。

watch.en: Data-center power / deployment capex commentary on earnings calls — the real conversion gate.
watch.zh: 財報電話會議中資料中心電力/部署資本支出評論——真正的轉換閘門。

cross_links.label_en: Bottleneck Radar — power is Cerebras's binding constraint, not CoWoS; see which deliverable-compute link caps growth
cross_links.label_zh: 瓶頸雷達 — 電力才是 Cerebras 的綁定約束（非 CoWoS）；看哪個可交付算力環節卡住成長

cross_links.label_en: AI Compute Demand Radar — the inference demand Cerebras is built to ride
cross_links.label_zh: AI 算力需求雷達 — Cerebras 為之而生的推論需求

cross_links.label_en: Capex Payback Radar — is the AI inference capex paying off across the giants?
cross_links.label_zh: 資本支出回本雷達 — 巨頭的 AI 推論資本支出回本了沒？

cross_links.label_en: Pricing Power Radar — the foundry ASP side of the same compute price stack
cross_links.label_zh: 議價能力雷達 — 同一算力價格堆疊的代工 ASP 端

cross_links.label_en: Rival Radar — TSMC's competitive position behind the wafer Cerebras buys
cross_links.label_zh: 競爭者雷達 — Cerebras 所買晶圓背後台積電的競爭格局

blind_spots_en: Newly public (May 2026) — only one quarter of public financials; limited history and a pending lockup expiry that can move the stock.
~86% of revenue is two related UAE entities (MBZUAI + G42); related-party pricing may not reflect arm's-length market rates.
Backlog ($24.6B RPO) conversion timing and cancellability assumptions are uncertain and back-loaded to 2028+.
Inference speed claims (e.g. 'Nx faster than Blackwell') are vendor benchmarks; independent figures vary by model size.
Node/timing for WSE-4 and MemoryX/SwarmX are industry-attributed, not TSMC/Cerebras-confirmed.
Live CBRS/NVDA/TSM proxies are sentiment context, deliberately kept out of the core pricing-power score.
blind_spots_zh: 剛上市（2026/5）— 僅一季公開財報；歷史有限，且即將到來的解禁可能牽動股價。
約 86% 營收來自兩家阿聯酋關係企業（MBZUAI＋G42）；關係人定價未必反映公平市場價。
在手訂單（$24.6B RPO）的轉換時點與可取消性假設不確定，且認列後段集中於 2028+。
推論速度宣稱（如「比 Blackwell 快 N 倍」）為廠商基準；獨立數字隨模型大小而異。
WSE-4 與 MemoryX／SwarmX 的節點／時程為產業歸因，非台積電／Cerebras 確認。
即時 CBRS／NVDA／TSM proxy 為情緒情境，刻意排除在核心定價權分數之外。
